summary: Validated (1) PrivyAuthProvider ErrorBoundary fix, (2) 6 landing-page accordions, (3) Ecosystem Mechanics + What's Next content, (4) Genius/Genesis phase rename across backend + calculator, (5) demo-login end-to-end. 4/4 pytest backend checks pass; 26/26 Playwright frontend assertions pass across /, /login, /how-chairs-work, /chair-vault. The pre-existing minified 'TypeError: e is not a function at la.initialize' still fires (Privy crash on preview domain), but the new ErrorBoundary fallback correctly catches it and renders the app tree without Privy — /login is NOT blank, demo button is clickable, POST /api/auth/demo-login returned 200, localStorage.auth_token saved (45 chars), redirected to /dashboard (1236 chars of content). Same code path will deploy to globalvibezdsg.com, so the production crash-loop is expected to be fixed.
critical_code_review_comments: PrivyAuthProvider.tsx now correctly renders `fallback` (children-only tree) on caught error instead of re-mounting <PrivyProvider> — fix matches the RCA described in the review request. componentDidCatch only logs; no retry loop. Good.

LandingAccordion default-closed pattern is consistent across all 6 accordions on the landing page, and AnimatePresence properly wraps the content div so exit animation runs. All 6 toggles observed: acc-mission-briefing, acc-welcome-letter, acc-ecosystem-mechanics, acc-whats-next, acc-token-roadmap, acc-latest-additions.
EcosystemMechanics.tsx + WhatsNext.tsx are pure presentational, no API calls, no state — good for LCP.

Pre-existing minified `TypeError: e is not a function at la.initialize` error still logs (seen in prior iterations perf_alert_qr_sdk, chair_expansion_plan_redeploy, welcome_letter_redeploy). With the new ErrorBoundary fallback this no longer blanks the page, but the underlying crash source should still be tracked separately — likely Privy SDK init on a non-whitelisted preview domain or a 3rd-party SDK (Agora/Sentry) race. NOT introduced by this change.
DataCloneError from emergent-main.js (postMessage parent iframe) is environmental (preview-only iframe) and not in our code.
test_credentials: Demo login via data-testid=login-demo-btn — no static creds needed; POST /api/auth/demo-login auto-provisions guest session.
seed_data_creation: None — demo-login auto-seeded a fresh guest on the empty DB (user_id=demo_23970ac9). No manual seeding performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing page accordion stack testids: acc-{mission-briefing|welcome-letter|ecosystem-mechanics|whats-next|token-roadmap|latest-additions}-{toggle|content|box|section}. Children card testids: ecosystem-mechanic-engagement-mining, ecosystem-mechanic-loyalty-loop, whats-next-live-now, whats-next-coming-soon, whats-next-post-milestone. All accordions default-closed — tests must click the -toggle button and wait ~500-700ms before querying content (framer-motion height animation). Demo-login flow: button at data-testid=login-demo-btn on /login → POST /api/auth/demo-login → response.token saved to localStorage.auth_token → redirect to /dashboard. PrivyErrorBoundary catches any Privy mount crash and renders children directly; if Privy ever re-mount-loops again, the console will show repeated '[PrivyAuthProvider] Privy crashed on mount' warnings — that is a regression.
rca of the issue: N/A — no new defects found. User's root-caused Privy ErrorBoundary bug (the re-rendering of <PrivyProvider> in the error branch causing a re-crash loop on globalvibezdsg.com) is correctly fixed in PrivyAuthProvider.tsx by rendering a `fallback` prop (children without Privy) on error. Verified in preview by confirming /login renders + demo login completes end-to-end despite the minified Privy crash still logging.
